PCOS is known for causing irregular periods and hormonal imbalances, leading to infertility. Since the ovaries look normal during laparoscopy, maybe the issue isn't structural but related to hormone levels. Also, PCOS is pretty common in women with unexplained infertility. On the other hand, endometriosis can sometimes be hard to detect, especially if it's mild or not visible during surgery. That makes sense because sometimes endometriosis can be asymptomatic or missed during exams.
